page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16-lssd is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is supported by a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD, while no GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core execution, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls within the average range. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 1133.96 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 38.41 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, memory write bandwidth for large blocks is a notable bottleneck, registering in the bottom 10% at 13.31 GB/sec.

The instance demonstrates average capabilities in database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. Large-scale LLM inference is a clear limitation, with 70B model prompt processing scoring in the bottom 10%. Given the dedicated AMD EPYC cores and integrated local NVMe storage, this instance offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads, text processing, and localized database operations that require fast storage access without requiring massive memory-to-CPU ratios.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. Multi-core performance, software compilation, and database operations fall in the average tier. A key hardware tradeoff is the large-block memory write bandwidth, which ranks in the bottom 10% at 13.31 GB/sec. Large LLM inference is also limited, with 70B model prompt processing scoring poorly. This instance is configured for general-purpose workloads, text processing, and database tasks requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for general workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16-lssd features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 402 GB NVMe SSD for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: An AMD EPYC general-purpose instance featuring dedicated vCPUs, balanced memory, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Average multi-core execution, software compilation, and database operations
Low large-block memory write bandwidth and limited large LLM inference capabilities
Suited for general-purpose applications and local database tasks
